title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCLXI. Paradoxes.
content:
[The following is quoted in Parkin's reply to Dr. Stukeley's second number of 'Origines Roystonian�,' 4to, London, 1748, p. vi.]

        Peter White will ne'er go right,
            Would you know the reason why?
        He follows his nose where'er he goes,
            And that stands all awry.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CCCLXI. Paradoxes.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
